task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is visible on the screen.
The OpenToonz window title bar or the Xsheet/timeline panel displays 'dwanko_run' or the character animation is visible in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: Select the animation frames in the Xsheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple cells in a column of the Xsheet (the vertical timeline grid) are highlighted or selected by the agent.

task_completion.description: Reverse the animation order in the Xsheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: The frame numbers inside the Xsheet cells are shown in descending order (e.g., going from a higher number down to 1), or a context menu with a 'Reverse' command is actively being clicked.

task_completion.description: Configure the render settings for PNG sequence and correct output directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' window is open. The 'Save In' or output path field is set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/reversed_animation/' and the file format is set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Render the reversed animation as a PNG sequence
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar/window is visible in OpenToonz, or a file manager window shows at least 10 rendered PNG files in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/reversed_animation/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenToonz GUI rather than terminal commands to reverse and render the animation
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows should be visible executing commands like 'ffmpeg', 'imagemagick', or python scripts to reverse or convert the images.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenToonz application, not a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not have the 'dwanko_run.tnz' file open in a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) to manually edit the XML structure to reverse the frames.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually copy and rename files to fake the output
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use the OpenToonz render functionality. There should be no evidence of the agent manually copying the original frames and renaming them in reverse order in the file manager or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Scene path: /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. Target output directory: /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/reversed_animation. The output must be a PNG sequence of at least 10 frames, and the total file size of the output must be at least 100 KB.
